_note: Simplified movement/breathing pacers for the Tai Chi tab, rendered by the SHARED form-model engine (web/js/formModel.js) — the same engine that powers Sports at Home. These are gentle tempo/breath guides, not instructed Tai Chi form (instructor-reviewed videos are the pending deliverable). Authored conservatively in the 240x340 joint model; movements use a wide, flowing range of motion. Only movements whose keyframes are verified ship here.
disclaimer: A simplified breathing-and-tempo pacer — move slowly through a comfortable, full range. Not a substitute for instructed Tai Chi form. Stop for chest pain, breathlessness, dizziness, or new joint pain.

movements.name: Opening — raise & lower arms

cues.Raise: Breathe in as the arms float all the way up overhead.
cues.Hold: Reach gently to the sky — tall and open.
cues.Lower: Breathe out as the arms sweep slowly down past the hips.
cues.Settle: Rest. Let the breath settle before the next cycle.

staticCues: Stand tall and relaxed, feet about hip-width, knees soft.
Float both arms forward and all the way up overhead as you breathe in.
Sweep the arms slowly down past your hips as you breathe out.
Keep shoulders soft and move through a full, comfortable range.

movements.name: Weight shift — side to side

cues.Left: Shift your weight fully across onto the left foot; let the arms float left.
cues.Hold left: Tall and balanced over the left leg — keep breathing.
cues.Right: Transfer slowly and fully across onto the right foot.
cues.Hold right: Steady over the right leg. Soft knees.

staticCues: Stand with feet wider than hip-width, knees soft.
Shift your weight fully onto one foot, bending that knee, arms drifting with you.
Transfer smoothly and fully across to the other foot.
Move through a wide, controlled range; keep support nearby if balance is unsure.
